sections.content:
Phase: auditor
Baseline SHA: 5e49a10e69820c7a03180ba31b63fee1cd7093a5
Started: 2026-03-23
Cycle ID: pev-2026-03-23-viz-typescript-refactor

Phase transitions:
- planning: 2026-03-23 — cycle created
- builder: 2026-03-23 — plan approved

sections.heading: Request
sections.content: Convert the viz frontend from vanilla JS in <script> tags to TypeScript ES modules, following the tiered plan in cortex::docs.viz-refactoring (4 tiers). ADR-006 describes the JS/TS scanner that will consume the result. End state: scannable by future tree-sitter JS/TS scanner — proper ESM imports, named exports, no implicit globals.

sections.heading: Scope
sections.content:
**Scope decision:** Single-feature. Frontend-only refactor of the viz dashboard.

**Modules affected:** All viz frontend JS files (`cortex/viz/static/js/`), `cortex/viz/static/index.html`, `cortex/viz/static/style.css`. No backend (server.py) changes. No Python scanner changes.

**What is IN scope:**
- Tier 1: Dead CSS removal + CSS custom properties for dark theme
- Tier 2: Extract shared view-utils module from duplicated code
- Tier 3: Decompose docs.js god object into 4 focused modules
- TypeScript conversion of all JS files to ES modules with strict mode
- Import map configuration for CDN dependencies
- tsconfig.json and build tooling (tsc only, no bundler)

**What is OUT of scope:**
- Tier 4 (server.py split into route groups) — deferred to a separate cycle
- Building the ADR-006 JS/TS scanner itself — this cycle produces the code that scanner will consume
- Bundler setup (esbuild, vite, etc.) — native browser ESM only
- Runtime behavior changes — this is a structural refactor, not a feature change

sections.heading: Architect Plan
sections.content: Shape Up pitch for the viz TypeScript/ESM conversion. Covers Tiers 1-3 from cortex::docs.viz-refactoring plus full TS/ESM conversion. Tier 4 (server.py) deferred to a separate cycle.\n\nSub-sections: problem, user-stories, design (solution sketch), constraints, affected-nodes, changelog-draft."

sections.heading: Problem
sections.content:
The viz frontend is ~5,700 lines of vanilla JS loaded via script tags with implicit load-order dependencies. Files communicate through global objects (App, Graph, Docs, etc.), making it impossible for ADR-006's tree-sitter JS scanner to build `validates` edges — the scanner explicitly skips non-ESM files. Additionally, `docs.js` at 2,500 lines is a god object with 8 responsibilities, `style.css` carries ~1,100 lines of dead light-mode CSS, and five files share ~300 lines of duplicated utility code with no shared abstraction.

This refactor converts the frontend to TypeScript ES modules so the codebase is (a) scannable by the future JS/TS scanner with proper import-based call graph resolution, (b) structurally decomposed so no single file exceeds ~600 lines, and (c) type-safe at module boundaries.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
1. **As a developer reading viz code**, I want each module to have a single responsibility and explicit imports, so that I can understand data flow without tracing global object mutations across files.
   - Acceptance: No file exceeds ~600 lines. Every cross-module dependency is an explicit ESM import. No implicit globals (window.App, window.Graph, etc.) remain in the TS source.

2. **As the future ADR-006 JS/TS scanner**, I want all viz modules to use ESM import/export syntax, so that I can build a name_map from imports and emit `validates` edges via call graph analysis.
   - Acceptance: Every `.ts` file uses `import { ... } from './module'` for cross-module references. `export` is used for all public API surfaces. The compiled `.js` output preserves ESM syntax (no CommonJS transform).

3. **As a viz user**, I want the dashboard to behave identically after the refactor, so that the structural change introduces no regressions.
   - Acceptance: All existing viz functionality works — graph view, list view, detail drawer, doc editor (tree, sections, Tiptap, Mermaid/Monaco diagrams, raw JSON), test explorer, workflow viewer. No new runtime errors in the browser console.

4. **As a developer maintaining the CSS**, I want dead light-mode rules removed and colors defined as CSS custom properties, so that theming is a single-place change.
   - Acceptance: style.css is reduced by ~1,000 lines. Repeated color hex values are replaced by custom properties defined in `:root`. Visual appearance is unchanged.

5. **As a developer working on workflow.js or tests.js**, I want shared utilities (HTML escape, Monaco init, filter panel, badge rendering) extracted into a common module, so that bug fixes apply everywhere.
   - Acceptance: `_esc()`, `_openSource()`, `_buildFilterPanel()`, Monaco AMD init, and path helpers exist in exactly one place (view-utils.ts). All consumers import from that module.

sections.heading: Solution Sketch
sections.content:
**Approach:** Big-bang cutover. All 7 JS files become 13 TS files in one branch. No intermediate hybrid state where some files are ESM and others are globals.

**Conversion sequence (within the branch):**

1. **Tier 1 — CSS cleanup.** Remove dead light-mode rules from style.css (~1,100 lines). Extract repeated color values into CSS custom properties on `:root`.

2. **Tier 2 — Extract view-utils.** Pull duplicated utilities (~300 lines across 5 files) into `ts/view-utils.ts`: HTML escape, Monaco AMD loader, filter panel builder, badge rendering, path helpers.

3. **Tier 3 — Decompose docs.js.** Split the 2,500-line god object into 4 focused modules: `doc-tree.ts` (folder tree + filters, ~200L), `doc-editor.ts` (section rendering + Tiptap lifecycle + section CRUD, ~600L), `doc-diagrams.ts` (Mermaid + Monaco diagram editor, ~300L), `doc-api.ts` (fetch/save/create + raw JSON, ~150L).

4. **TS/ESM conversion.** Convert all files to TypeScript. Each module exports named functions/classes (no default exports). Cross-module references use ESM imports. A shared `types.ts` defines core interfaces (CortexNode, DocSection, Edge, etc.).

**Target module map (13 files in `cortex/viz/static/ts/`):**

| File | Responsibility | ~Lines |
|---|---|---|
| app.ts | Orchestrator, routing, state | ~800 |
| graph.ts | Cytoscape lifecycle | ~350 |
| list.ts | Node table | ~85 |
| detail.ts | Detail drawer | ~300 |
| view-utils.ts | Shared utilities (Tier 2) | ~300 |
| doc-tree.ts | Folder tree + filter | ~200 |
| doc-editor.ts | Section rendering + Tiptap | ~600 |
| doc-diagrams.ts | Mermaid + Monaco diagram editor | ~300 |
| doc-api.ts | Doc CRUD + raw JSON | ~150 |
| tests.ts | Test explorer | ~850 |
| workflow.ts | Workflow viewer | ~650 |
| types.ts | Shared interfaces | ~100 |
| monaco-facade.ts | Typed wrapper for AMD Monaco | ~50 |

**CDN dependency strategy:** Use an `<script type="importmap">` in index.html mapping bare specifiers to CDN URLs. Code writes `import cytoscape from 'cytoscape'`; the browser resolves via the import map. Monaco remains as a `<script>` tag (AMD, not ESM-compatible) with a typed facade (`monaco-facade.ts`) providing ambient declarations.

**Build tooling:** `tsc` only (no bundler). `tsconfig.json` targets ES2022 modules, ES2022 language. Compiled `.js` files go to `cortex/viz/static/js/` and are served directly by FastAPI's StaticFiles mount. The TS source lives in `cortex/viz/static/ts/`.

**Type depth:** Strict interfaces for core entities (CortexNode, DocSection, Edge, StalenessCause) in `types.ts`. Tiptap and Mermaid interactions use `any` or minimal interface stubs rather than pulling in full type packages.

**Affected files:**
- `cortex/viz/static/index.html` — replace `<script>` tags with `<script type="module">` + import map
- `cortex/viz/static/style.css` — dead CSS removal + custom properties
- `cortex/viz/static/js/*.js` — all 7 files replaced by compiled output from TS
- `cortex/viz/static/ts/*.ts` — 13 new TypeScript source files (NEW)
- `cortex/viz/static/tsconfig.json` — TypeScript configuration (NEW)
- `pyproject.toml` — possibly add a tsc build step note or dev dependency mention

**Edge cases the Builder should watch for:**
- **Monaco AMD vs ESM tension.** Monaco editor loads via AMD `require()`, not ESM. The facade must handle the async load and expose a typed API. Existing code has an AMD init IIFE duplicated across workflow.js, tests.js, and docs.js — all three need to delegate to the facade.
- **Cytoscape event handlers.** graph.js binds Cytoscape events that call back into App and Detail globals. These become imports — but circular import risk exists if app.ts imports graph.ts and graph.ts imports app.ts. May need an event bus or callback registration pattern.
- **Tiptap editor lifecycle.** docs.js creates/destroys Tiptap instances on every section render. The decomposed doc-editor.ts must own this lifecycle carefully — Tiptap state must not leak across re-renders.
- **DOM element ID assumptions.** All modules query the DOM by hardcoded IDs. During conversion, ensure IDs remain stable — index.html is the source of truth for element IDs.
- **Script load order.** The import map + `type="module"` approach changes load order from synchronous `<script>` to async module resolution. Verify that DOMContentLoaded timing still works correctly.

sections.heading: Constraints
sections.content:
**Rabbit holes — do not go here:**
- Do NOT introduce a bundler (esbuild, vite, webpack, parcel). Native browser ESM is sufficient for this codebase size. Bundler setup is a separate future decision.
- Do NOT split server.py (Tier 4). Backend is out of scope.
- Do NOT add full type definitions for Tiptap, Mermaid, or Cytoscape. Use ambient declarations or `any` for third-party library interactions. The goal is structural conversion, not exhaustive type coverage.
- Do NOT refactor the DOM manipulation patterns or event handling architecture. Keep the existing imperative DOM style — just make it type-safe and modular. A reactive framework migration is a separate discussion.
- Do NOT change any API endpoints or request/response shapes. The refactor is purely frontend structural.

**No-gos:**
- No runtime behavior changes. If manual testing reveals any functional difference from the current JS version, that is a bug to fix before merge.
- No new npm dependencies installed locally. All third-party code comes from CDN via import map.
- No CommonJS output. The tsconfig must produce ESM output that the browser loads directly.

**Trade-offs accepted:**
- `any` types for infrequently-used API response shapes and third-party library interactions. Strict typing is reserved for core domain objects (CortexNode, DocSection, edges) and module boundaries.
- Big-bang cutover means the branch cannot be merged incrementally. This is accepted because the alternative (hybrid global+ESM) creates a more complex intermediate state than either pure-global or pure-ESM.
- Monaco stays on AMD/CDN with a typed facade rather than being converted to ESM. Monaco's ESM build requires a bundler, which conflicts with the no-bundler constraint.

**Test budget:**
- No automated frontend tests exist today and adding a test framework (Jest, Vitest) is out of scope. Validation is manual: load the dashboard, exercise each view (graph, list, detail, docs editor, tests, workflow), verify no console errors and identical behavior.
- The Builder should document a manual test checklist covering each view's core interactions.
- After merge, the ADR-006 scanner (when built) serves as the structural validation — if it can parse all 13 modules and build a name_map from their imports, the ESM conversion succeeded.

sections.heading: Affected Nodes
sections.content:
This cycle affects frontend files that are not currently indexed by cortex (JS files are not scanned by the Python AST scanner). The only indexed node directly affected is:

- `cortex::cortex.viz.server` — index.html is served by this module's static mount; script tag changes affect how the frontend loads but not the server code itself.

The primary output of this cycle (13 TS files) will become scannable by the future ADR-006 JS/TS scanner, at which point they will appear as indexed nodes with `validates` edges.

sections.heading: Changelog Draft
sections.content:
### Changed
- Viz frontend converted from vanilla JS (7 files, global objects) to TypeScript ES modules (13 files, explicit imports/exports)
- `docs.js` god object (2,500L) decomposed into 4 focused modules: doc-tree, doc-editor, doc-diagrams, doc-api
- Shared utilities extracted into view-utils.ts, eliminating ~300 lines of duplication across 5 files
- Dead light-mode CSS removed (~1,100 lines); color values consolidated into CSS custom properties
- CDN dependencies loaded via import map instead of ordered script tags
- TypeScript strict interfaces added for core domain types (CortexNode, DocSection, Edge)

### Added
- `cortex/viz/static/ts/` — TypeScript source directory (13 modules)
- `cortex/viz/static/tsconfig.json` — TypeScript compiler configuration (ES2022 target, ESM output)
- `types.ts` — shared type definitions for viz domain objects
- `monaco-facade.ts` — typed wrapper for AMD-loaded Monaco editor
- `view-utils.ts` — shared utilities (HTML escape, Monaco init, filter panel, badge rendering)

sections.heading: Change Set
sections.content:
33 files changed, 13,996 insertions, 8,981 deletions

**New files (TS source):**
- cortex/viz/static/ts/app.ts, detail.ts, doc-api.ts, doc-diagrams.ts, doc-editor.ts, doc-tree.ts, graph.ts, list.ts, list-source-panel.ts, monaco-facade.ts, tests.ts, types.ts, view-utils.ts, workflow.ts
- cortex/viz/static/ts/tsconfig.json, .gitignore

**Modified files (compiled JS output):**
- cortex/viz/static/js/app.js, detail.js, doc-api.js, doc-diagrams.js, doc-editor.js, doc-tree.js, graph.js, list.js, list-source-panel.js, monaco-facade.js, tests.js, types.js, view-utils.js, workflow.js

**Deleted files:**
- cortex/viz/static/js/docs.js (decomposed into doc-api, doc-editor, doc-tree, doc-diagrams)

**Modified files (other):**
- cortex/viz/static/css/style.css (CSS custom properties, dead CSS removal)
- cortex/viz/static/index.html (import map, ESM script tags)

sections.heading: Builder Manifest
sections.content:
**Builder — COMPLETE (initial build + 5 fix rounds)**

**Initial build** (incarnation 2-3): Complete viz frontend TypeScript/ESM conversion. 14 TS modules with strict typing.

**Fix round 1** (app+list+detail+types, 10 fixes): _updateSinceUI, _loadRecentShas, _renderShaDropdown, SUBTYPE_LABELS, ColResize wiring, doVerify stalenessMap, Monaco model dispose, clearCauseCache(), CortexNode fields, "Edit in Docs" doc selection.

**Fix round 2** (docs critical, 5 fixes): XSS in renderMarkdown, Tiptap double-escaping, raw source toggle validate/apply, save re-fetch + feedback, inline validation errors.

**Fix round 3** (docs important, 10 fixes): nestRawSections, full Tiptap toolbar, toolbar active states, contentForEditor markdown→HTML, rich filter panel, mermaid SVG sizing, mermaid autocomplete, IntersectionObserver config, modal move dialog, rename-sections error check.

**Fix round 4** (tests critical, 5 fixes): API endpoint, T1 detail view, fixture actions, per-item run button, step click-to-jump.

**Fix round 5** (tests important, 8 fixes): Tier/result grouping order, group run buttons, clear filters, skipped icon, step count chip, input/output metadata, legacy coverage, Monaco model leak.

**Total: 38 fixes across 5 rounds. All TS compiles clean. 281 Python tests pass (12 pre-existing failures).**

sections.heading: Auditor Impact Report
sections.content:
**Auditor incarnation 1 — cycle-specific review complete**

**Nodes reviewed:** 14 (all cycle-specific)
**Nodes marked clean:** 14
**Docs updated:** 5 sections across 2 documents
**Needs fix:** none

**Doc updates made:**
- `viz.design::architecture` — rewrote file tree for 14 TS modules, added module loading + docs.js decomposition
- `viz.design::sequence-diagram` — updated page load for ESM/import map
- `viz.design::decision-log` — added TypeScript/ESM and CSS custom properties decisions
- `viz-refactoring::overview` — reflects completed state
- `viz-refactoring::priority-summary` — Tiers 1-3 marked Done

**Key finding:** This cycle changed only frontend files (TS/JS/HTML/CSS) that are not indexed by the Python AST scanner. Zero Python code nodes were made stale by the refactor. All 167 remaining stale nodes are collateral from prior PEV cycles (semantic search, ADR-010, ADR-013, ADR-014, checkout tool, PEV nexus docs, ADR-012, templates). Not in scope for this cycle.
